reason: Confirmed requirements, acceptance criteria, migration boundary, and out-of-scope items for the Home Riverpod/hooks refactor.
Implementation guidance for AsyncNotifier/AsyncValue screen state, refresh, pagination, favorite mutation, and hook usage.
Coexistence guidance for introducing Riverpod while keeping existing provider-based LoginViewModel ownership.
Current project state ownership rules and the deliberate boundary exception for this migration.
Existing hook migration guardrails and lifecycle expectations that this feature-boundary migration must satisfy.
Project quality rules for request wrappers, navigation consistency, UI verification, and avoiding unrelated architecture churn.
Shared Request/BaseModelEntity contracts that Home data fetching and favorite mutations must preserve.
